summary: Global equity markets are navigating a cautiously mixed mood, with investors balancing resilience in AI-driven technology stocks against escalating geopolitical stress in the Middle East, as the Iran conflict continues to dominate risk sentiment. On the macro front, U.S. job openings surged to 7.6 million in April — the highest level in nearly two years — signaling persistent labor market tightness that complicates the Federal Reserve's path toward rate cuts, while the AI infrastructure boom is being flagged as an additional inflationary pressure. In equities, HPE surged alongside Dell on a massive guidance hike tied to enterprise AI spending, Palo Alto Networks is in focus ahead of earnings, and institutional investors are trimming positions in high-flying AI names after significant year-to-date gains, suggesting some profit-taking at elevated valuations. The dominant risk factors include the Iran war's disruption to Middle East energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a projectile strike on a vessel at Iraq's Umm Qasr port, and spillover effects on emerging markets — particularly India's central bank, which faces a razor-edge policy dilemma balancing inflation, growth, and rupee stability. In the short term, markets are likely to remain volatile and headline-driven, with any deterioration or de-escalation in Iran-Israel tensions serving as the primary binary catalyst, while strong AI sector fundamentals provide a partial offset to broader geopolitical and inflation headwinds.
